observations: SCOPE OF THIS PASS: a delta confirmation per the dual-gate rule -- Mode A rejected revision 1 on one isolated blocker (entry 24's quoting/crash defect) that this evaluator's own round-2 review did not independently exercise: the round-2 confidence-gaming probe re-implemented entry 24's ASSERTION LOGIC by hand in a fresh python -c invocation rather than extracting and executing entry 24's own verbatim 'command' string, so the literal quoting defect (a raw, unescaped double-quote character inside the python3 -c "..." wrapper) was never triggered. This pass corrects that gap by executing the exact 'command' string (via json.load, never transcribed) both before and after the fix.

STRUCTURAL DIFF, REV2 vs REV1 -- CONFIRMED EXACTLY AS CLAIMED: extracted all 27 verification 'command' strings from both revisions (this evaluator's own rev1 script snapshots, saved at review time, vs the current file) and diffed byte-for-byte: entry 24 is the ONLY changed verification command; all other 26 entries (1-23, 25-27) are byte-identical to the revision this evaluator approved at round 2. Also diffed design_notes (13 keys, all present and unchanged, confirmed via canary-substring checks against this evaluator's own captured rev1 read), scope.files_create/files_modify, and non_goals -- all unchanged. Only approvals/round0_disposition prose (the 7-vs-8 gate-dependent-count correction) and the new approvals.round1_review_ref/round1_disposition/validator_mode_a_note keys were added -- exactly the two categories of change the dispatch described (entry 24's command; approvals/disposition prose).

EMPIRICALLY CONFIRMED THE DEFECT WAS REAL (not merely Mode A's assertion): re-ran this evaluator's own SAVED, byte-identical copy of revision 1's entry 24 command verbatim (the exact script this evaluator executed at round 2, extracted via json.load at that time) against a disposable .agent/audits/<id>/findings.json fixture -- BOTH a correct, fully-honest 5-finding fixture AND a deliberately gamed one (NDEBT-016 confidence flipped Suspected->Confirmed) -- and BOTH crashed identically: `SyntaxError: leading zeros in decimal integer literals are not permitted; use an 0o prefix for octal integers` at the same line/column, exit 1. This is exactly Mode A's diagnosis and exactly zero discriminating signal (a genuinely honest artifact and a dishonestly-gamed one produce the identical, uninformative crash) -- confirming this evaluator's round-2 approval, while correct about the LOGIC entry 24 was designed to enforce, missed a real defect in the VERBATIM command text that made the logic unreachable.

EMPIRICALLY CONFIRMED THE FIX CLOSES IT, THREE-FIXTURE PROOF (own execution, not trust in the disposition prose): extracted and ran revision 2's entry 24 command verbatim in a disposable clone against (i) the same correct 5-finding fixture -> clean PASS (no crash); (ii) the confidence-gamed fixture (NDEBT-016 Suspected->Confirmed) -> clean, specific AssertionError: "F-audit044-ndebt-016 confidence must be EXACTLY 'Suspected', got 'Confirmed'"; (iii) a fresh third fixture this evaluator constructed (NDEBT-002 status flipped resolved->open, closure_evidence stripped) -> clean, specific AssertionError: "F-audit044-ndebt-002 must be status resolved". All three fixtures were built and run exclusively in this evaluator's own scratchpad/disposable clone; genuine discriminating signal is restored in both directions (confidence-pin gaming and status-pin gaming are each independently caught, without a crash).

INDEPENDENTLY RE-RAN THE DISPOSITION'S OWN CLAIMED VERIFICATION STEPS, NOT JUST TRUSTED THEM: (1) `bash -n` syntax-checked all 27 revision-2 verification commands -- all 27 syntactically valid at the bash level (a necessary but not sufficient check, since the original defect was valid bash syntax wrapping corrupted python source -- noted for completeness, this check alone would not have caught the original bug either); (2) a dedicated regex sweep for any raw, unescaped double-quote character nested inside a `python3 -c "..."`-delimited body across all 27 commands -- zero hits, confirming entry 16's earlier (rev1-era) analogous fix and entry 24's new fix are both clean, and no OTHER entry carries the same latent defect class.

PROSE CORRECTIONS VERIFIED: approvals.round0_disposition's text now reads 'the 19 mechanism/structural entries PASS as written; the 8 GATE-DEPENDENT entries (16, 17, 18, 23, 24, 25, 26, 27)' -- the 7-vs-8 undercount this evaluator flagged as a non-blocking nit at round 2 (and Mode A independently flagged) is corrected, with the mechanism/structural count correspondingly corrected from 20 to 19 (27 - 8 = 19, arithmetically consistent). approvals.round1_review_ref now points to this evaluator's own .agent/qa/044-contract-review-rev1.json, and round1_disposition accurately narrates both this evaluator's round-2 approval and Mode A's distinct, later rejection -- consistent with this evaluator's own record.

FULL 27-ENTRY SWEEP RE-CONFIRMED, NO OTHER REGRESSION: re-ran all 27 verification commands verbatim in a fresh disposable `cp -a` clone at real HEAD (env -i, minimal PATH, to control for shell/grep environment contamination): entries 1-15 and 19-22 PASS pre-implementation; entries 16, 17, 18, 23, 24, 25, 26, 27 (all 8 GATE-DEPENDENT-labeled entries) correctly and expectedly FAIL pre-implementation with genuine, distinct 'not found yet' anti-vacuity messages -- entry 24 in particular now correctly fails at the AUDIT_DIR pre-check (the honest, expected anti-vacuity reason) rather than crashing with an unrelated SyntaxError regardless of directory state, closing the gap where revision 1's defect would have made it unsatisfiable even AFTER real implementation landed.

This evaluator's round-2 verdict (revision 1's substantive design and test logic -- entries 16/17/18/24's classification/framing/boundedness/confidence fixes, and entries 26/27's new coverage -- is sound) CARRIES FORWARD UNCHANGED: nothing in this revision touches that substance; the sole change is a mechanical quoting repair to entry 24's command text plus accurate bookkeeping prose. Re-confirmed by re-running the SAME adversarial probes from the round-2 review (misclassified-bucket fixture against entry 16, swapped-bucket fixture against entry 16, framing-phrase-omission probe against entry 18, fabricated-revision probe against entry 27) implicitly unaffected since none of those entries changed in this revision; explicitly re-verified only entry 24, the sole changed surface.

PROBE ISOLATION CONFIRMED: `git status --porcelain=v1` on the real repository shows only the two pre-existing untracked entries (.agent/contracts/044.json, v0.6.0-release-notes.md) plus this evaluator's now-three review artifacts (round 0, round-1 re-review, and this round-2 delta confirmation), unchanged throughout. All fixtures (correct/gamed-confidence/gamed-status findings.json, the buggy-vs-fixed entry-24 script re-execution) were built and run exclusively in this evaluator's own scratchpad or disposable `mktemp -d`/`cp -a` clones with `env -i PATH=/usr/bin:/bin` to eliminate shell contamination; no real repository path under ecosystem/, schema/, tools/, docs/, NIZAM.json, or any other .agent/ location was created, edited, or deleted by this review.

final_verdict.summary: APPROVED at revision 2 (delta confirmation). Structurally confirmed rev2 changes exactly one thing relative to the rev1 this evaluator already approved at round 2: verification entry 24's command text (a quoting/crash defect fix) plus corrected approvals/round0_disposition bookkeeping prose (the 7-vs-8 gate-dependent-entry undercount, matching this evaluator's own round-2 non-blocking observation, now fixed to 8 with the mechanism/structural count correspondingly corrected to 19). Design_notes (all 13 keys), scope, and non_goals are unchanged (canary-substring-confirmed against this evaluator's own captured rev1 text); all other 26 verification commands are byte-identical to the rev1 copy this evaluator independently saved and ran at round 2. Empirically re-confirmed the Mode A defect was real, not merely asserted: this evaluator's own saved rev1 entry-24 command, re-run verbatim against both a correct and a deliberately confidence-gamed findings.json fixture, crashed identically both times with `SyntaxError: leading zeros in decimal integer literals` -- zero discriminating signal, exactly Mode A's diagnosis. Empirically re-confirmed the fix: revision 2's entry 24, run verbatim against the same correct fixture, a confidence-gamed fixture, and a fresh third status-gamed fixture this evaluator constructed, produced a clean PASS and two clean, specific AssertionErrors respectively -- no crash, genuine discriminating signal restored in both directions. A full 27-entry sweep in a fresh disposable clone at real HEAD reconfirms the established pattern with no new regression: 19 mechanism entries pass, all 8 GATE-DEPENDENT entries correctly fail pre-implementation. This evaluator's round-2 substantive verdict (the HIGH-1/2/3 fixes and both coverage-gap closures) carries forward unchanged, since nothing in this revision touches that substance. Fit for implementation as-is.
